marketState: neutral - fairly valued, with a bearish short-term technical tilt and a low-conviction long-term upside bias
summary: Front-month NG is trading essentially at fair value ($2.911 vs $2.923 model fair, spread only -$0.012), so there is no mispricing edge in either direction right now. Seasonality is flagged Low reliability this week (59pp 10-90 spread) and mildly bearish overall - price sits -7.7pp below its typical seasonal path, only 38% odds of being higher in 3-4 weeks, and a typical 4-week move of -1.4%. Weather demand is running +11.3% (1-7D) and +6.2% (8-16D) above the 5-year normal, seasonally supportive, but the 24h demand trend just dropped sharply (-15.3) and daily/weekly technicals show a bearish trend with a 50<200 EMA death cross and negative MACD. Storage sits at a +180 Bcf surplus vs the 5-year average with a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise this week, which caps upside, while managed-money COT positioning is extremely net short (8th percentile of the 5-year range) - a contrarian setup that argues against chasing further weakness. Futures curve data was unavailable in this snapshot, so curve structure could not be checked.

longTerm.rationale: Managed-money is at an 8th-percentile net-short extreme (contrarian bullish for short-covering) and the fair-value curve rises from $2.923 now to $3.011 by +21D, implying the model expects a firmer market into the fall shoulder season; weather demand also remains above 5-year normal.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained close below $2.75, a further deepening of the managed-money net-short position, or the storage surplus widening materially beyond +180 Bcf vs 5y would invalidate this long-term long thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: 4h/1d/1w technical trend is bearish with a 50<200 EMA death cross and negative daily/weekly MACD, near-term weather demand just fell -15.3 in 24h despite still running above the 5-year normal, and front price sits exactly at fair value, offering no fundamental floor to lean on for a bounce.
shortTerm.invalidation: A reclaim and hold above $3.05 with the EMA cross flipping bullish and MACD turning positive on the 4h/1d, or a renewed spike in near-term weather demand, would invalidate the short.
